description: A designed rapid-response rebuttal card on a dark navy background. At the top is the white DHS seal and bold white serif 'ICE' wordmark. Below, in white serif text, it paraphrases a news report being debunked: 'A 19-year-old Bangladeshi migrant reportedly died in ICE custody in El Paso, according to witnesses. A migrant has reportedly died at the Fort Bliss detention center. This would be the first death recorded since the facility opened.' Centered is an embedded screenshot of a Univision 'noticias EL PASO' news webpage with the Spanish headline 'Migrante de bangladesh de 19 anos habria muerto bajo custodia de ICE en El Paso, segun testigos' and subhead 'Presunta muerte de migrante en centro de detencion de Fort Bliss. Seria el primer fallecimiento registrado desde su apertura,' tagged 'NOTICIAS / INMIGRACION.' The bottom features a torn-newspaper texture strip over which is set a very large bold navy meme-style word 'NOPE.' followed by a row of red chevron arrows pointing right. Propaganda technique: directly attacks/discredits press coverage as false ('NOPE'), pairing an official seal with sarcastic internet-meme typography to delegitimize journalism critical of detention conditions (the companion tweet calls it 'dangerous journalism' and 'lies'). Target audience: ICE's social-media followers and the broader public, to preempt and discredit reporting on a detainee death.
notable_text: ICE; A 19-year-old Bangladeshi migrant reportedly died in ICE custody in El Paso, according to witnesses... This would be the first death recorded since the facility opened; Migrante de bangladesh de 19 anos habria muerto bajo custodia de ICE en El Paso, segun testigos; NOPE.
